The plan sets economy-wide targets and identifies priority sectors, including automotive, semiconductors, pharmaceuticals, chemicals, energy, aerospace, agro-industry, consumer goods, transport, water, textiles and tourism.
It also includes quantified commitments, such as $10 billion in semiconductor investment relocation."},{"url":"https://www.bu.edu/gdp/2026/06/23/plan-mexico-legal-risks-and-opportunities-of-mexicos-public-sector-led-industrial-strategy/","title":"Plan México: Legal Risks (and Opportunities) of Mexico’s Public Sector-Led Industrial Strategy | Global Development Policy Center","content":"At the same time, Mexican President Claudia Sheinbaum is helming an economy-wide “strategy for economic and sustainable development for a shared prosperity” known as Plan México.
Launched in January 2025, Plan México aims to significantly scale up public works invest­ment in energy, water, transportation, professional and technical education and housing.
National development banks like the Banco Nacional de Obras y Servicios Públicos (BANOBRAS) and the Nacional Financiera (NAFIN) are tasked with providing affordable financing for Mexican firms, especially those with government contracts for public works projects."},{"url":"https://www.bakerinstitute.org/sites/default/files/2025-10/11062025-Mexico%20Country%20Outlook%202026_Final%20Edition.pdf","title":"Mexico Country Outlook 2026","content":"Nov 6, 2025 — Even in an optimistic scenario, economic growth is likely to hover between 1.3% and 1.5% in 2026 (Figure 1).
